# Design Token Mapping Reference

> **Auto-generated** by `build-tokens.js`. Do not edit by hand.
> Source: `export-from-figma/tokens-from-ts.json` + `export-from-figma/animation.json`.
> Re-run `pnpm build:tokens` (or `pnpm sync:tokens`) to refresh.

This document is the single source of truth for coding agents: every token that
exists in the Pampered Chef design system, its resolved value, and when to use
it. All tokens listed here are available as SCSS variables in `build/scss/`
and as CSS custom properties in `build/css/`.

## Token Structure Overview

The design system is organized into the following groups:

- **Tokens** — Semantic color tokens (text, buttons, border, icon, surface, background, overlay, graphics, focus-fx)
- **Numeric Tokens** — Spacing, radius, size scales
- **Fonts** — Font families and variants
- **Elevation** — Multi-layer box shadows and depth
- **Responsive/Desktop** — Desktop typography and section spacing
- **Responsive/Mobile** — Mobile typography and section spacing
- **Focus** — Focus ring styles
- **Motion** — Animation durations, delays, and easings
- **Breakpoints** — Responsive breakpoints
- **Ratios** — Aspect ratios

## Web Code Syntax Format

All tokens use the format: `$collection-category-property-variant-state`

Examples:

- `$color-buttons-primary-default`
- `$spacing-md`
- `$desktop-typography-text-md-regular`
- `$size-height-sm`
- `$radius-md`

Each token entry below is rendered as:

```
- `$scss-name` — `resolved value` — Description (when provided)
```

Typography tokens are composite — include them with the matching SCSS mixin
(same name as the token), e.g. `@include desktop-typography-text-md-regular;`.

---

## Color Tokens (`build/scss/_tokens.scss`)

### Text

- `$color-text-default-primary` — `#2e3030` — Use for primary text, such as sentence case headings, field labels and buttons.
- `$color-text-default-primary-inverted` — `#ffffff` — Use for primary text, such as sentence case headings, field labels and buttons on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-text-default-disabled-on-surface` — `#6b6e70` — Use for text in a disabled state but on color surfaces.
- `$color-text-default-secondary` — `#45474a` — Used for secondary text such as body paragraphs.
- `$color-text-default-tertiary` — `#6b6e70` — Use for less important texts such as meta-data, breadcrumbs, input field placeholder and helper text.
- `$color-text-default-disabled` — `#a3a6a8` — Use for text in a disabled state.
- `$color-text-accent-default` — `#2b7a87` — Used for highlight titles or words on a text block.
- `$color-text-accent-on-surface` — `#1a5961` — Use it when the accent text is over a teal-tinted surface (e.g. the label on a Secondary or Tertiary Button on hover state).
- `$color-text-accent-high-contrast` — `#0f3d42` — Use it when the accent text needs a higher-contrast against a surface (e.g. the label on a Secondary or Tertiary Button on pressed state).
- `$color-text-error-default` — `#cc1700` — Use for critical text, such as input field error messaging.
- `$color-text-error-high-contrast` — `#7a0f00` — Use for critical text on lighter red surfaces.
- `$color-text-error-high-contrast-inverted` — `#ff9487` — Use for critical text on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-text-info-default` — `#1c78cc` — Use for informational text on inline content.
- `$color-text-info-high-contrast` — `#124a7d` — Use for informational text on lighter blue surfaces.
- `$color-text-info-high-contrast-inverted` — `#73bffc` — Use for informational text on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-text-success-default` — `#298538` — Used for success text on inline content.
- `$color-text-success-high-contrast` — `#1c5929` — Use for success text on lighter surfaces.
- `$color-text-success-high-contrast-inverted` — `#69db7d` — Use for success text on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-text-warning-default` — `#c74f00` — Use for warning text on inline content.
- `$color-text-warning-high-contrast` — `#803300` — Use for warning text on lighter orange surfaces.
- `$color-text-warning-high-contrast-inverted` — `#ffa84d` — Use for warning text on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-text-emphasis-default` — `#73296e` — Used for emphasis text on inline content.
- `$color-text-emphasis-high-contrast` — `#541c4f` — Use for emphasis text on lighter purple surfaces.
- `$color-text-emphasis-high-contrast-inverted` — `#bd87ba` — Use for emphasis text on inverted surfaces.

### Buttons

- `$color-buttons-primary-default` — `#fac761` — Used for the fill for Primary Buttons on Default state.
- `$color-buttons-primary-hovered` — `#f5b83d`
- `$color-buttons-primary-pressed` — `#eda31c` — Used for the fill for Primary Buttons on Pressed state.
- `$color-buttons-primary-disabled` — `#c9cccf` — Used for the fill for Primary Buttons on Disabled state.
- `$color-buttons-primary-default-inverted` — `#ffffff`
- `$color-buttons-primary-hovered-inverted` — `#f0f7f7`
- `$color-buttons-primary-pressed-inverted` — `#e0edf0`
- `$color-buttons-primary-disabled-inverted` — `#c9cccf`
- `$color-buttons-secondary-default` — `#ffffff0a` — Used for the fill for Secondary Buttons on Default state.
- `$color-buttons-secondary-hovered` — `#f0f7f7`
- `$color-buttons-secondary-pressed` — `#e0edf0` — Used for the fill for Secondary Buttons on Pressed state.
- `$color-buttons-secondary-disabled` — `#e8e8eb` — Used for the fill for Secondary Buttons on Disabled state.
- `$color-buttons-secondary-default-inverted` — `#0505050a`
- `$color-buttons-secondary-hovered-inverted` — `#ffffff17`
- `$color-buttons-secondary-pressed-inverted` — `#ffffff26`
- `$color-buttons-secondary-disabled-inverted` — `#ffffff0a`
- `$color-buttons-destructive-default` — `#cc1700` — Used for the fill for Destructive Buttons on Default state.
- `$color-buttons-destructive-hovered` — `#a81400` — Used for the fill for Destructive Buttons on Hover state.
- `$color-buttons-destructive-pressed` — `#7a0f00` — Used for the fill for Destructive Buttons on Pressed state.

### Border

- `$color-border-button-default` — `#2b7a87` — Used for the border of a Secondary Button on Default state.
- `$color-border-button-hovered` — `#1a5961` — Used for the border of a Secondary Button on Hover state.
- `$color-border-button-pressed` — `#0f3d42` — Used for the border of a Secondary Button on Pressed state.
- `$color-border-button-disabled` — `#a3a6a8` — Used for the border of a Secondary Button on Disabled state.
- `$color-border-button-default-inverted` — `#e0edf0`
- `$color-border-button-hovered-inverted` — `#d9e8ed`
- `$color-border-button-pressed-inverted` — `#bfe3e8`
- `$color-border-button-disabled-inverted` — `#a3a6a8`
- `$color-border-focus-default` — `#2e3030` — Use for default focus ring.
- `$color-border-focus-accent` — `#2b7a87` — Use for the focus ring of highlighted components.
- `$color-border-focus-accent-inverted` — `#bfe3e8` — Use for the focus ring of highlighted components on inverted surface.
- `$color-border-focus-error` — `#7a0f00` — Use for the focus ring of error state components.
- `$color-border-focus-error-inverted` — `#fa6652` — Use for the focus ring of error state components on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-border-scroll-active` — `#2b7a87` — Used for on the Active handle of a Scrollbar component.
- `$color-border-scroll-inactive` — `#d6dbdb` — Used for on the Inactive handle of a Scrollbar component.
- `$color-border-input-default` — `#b5b8ba` — Used for the border of an Input
- `$color-border-input-default-inverted` — `#c9cccf`
- `$color-border-input-hover` — `#697073`
- `$color-border-input-hover-inverted` — `#e3ebed`
- `$color-border-input-active` — `#2e3030` — Used for the border of an Input
- `$color-border-input-active-inverted` — `#61bac7`
- `$color-border-input-disabled` — `#d6dbdb` — Used for the border of an Input
- `$color-border-input-error` — `#cc1700` — Used for the border of an Input
- `$color-border-input-error-inverted` — `#ff9487`
- `$color-border-icon-button-default` — `#b5b8ba`
- `$color-border-icon-button-hovered` — `#2b7a87`
- `$color-border-icon-button-active` — `#1a5961`
- `$color-border-divider-subtle` — `#e3ebed` — Used for dividers between sections
- `$color-border-divider-strong` — `#b2bdbd` — Used for dividers with higher emphasis (e.g. dividers over tinted backgrounds
- `$color-border-divider-inverted` — `#ffffff` — Used for inverted dividers on solid backgrounds.
- `$color-border-accent-default` — `#2b7a87` — Used for accented icons or containers with stroke.
- `$color-border-accent-strong` — `#0f3d42` — Used for accented icons or containers with stroke that requires higher contrast.
- `$color-border-info-solid` — `#248ce6` — Color border for solid info elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-info-subtle` — `#73bffc` — Color border for subtle info elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-success-solid` — `#40bf57` — Color border for solid success elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-success-subtle` — `#8ce899` — Color border for subtle success elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-warning-solid` — `#fc7d14` — Color border for solid warning elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-warning-subtle` — `#ffbf78` — Color border for subtle warning elements (banners, containers).
- `$color-border-error-solid` — `#cc1700` — Color border for solid error elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-border-error-subtle` — `#ffb3a8` — Color border for subtle error elements (banners, containers).

### Links

- `$color-links-default` — `#2b7a87` — Used for links on default state.
- `$color-links-hovered` — `#1a5961`
- `$color-links-disabled` — `#a3a6a8` — Used for links on a disabled state.
- `$color-links-pressed` — `#0f3d42` — Used for links on pressed state.
- `$color-links-default-inverted` — `#e0edf0`
- `$color-links-hovered-inverted` — `#d9e8ed`
- `$color-links-pressed-inverted` — `#bfe3e8`
- `$color-links-disabled-inverted` — `#6b6e70`
- `$color-links-default-on-surface` — `#1a5961` — Used for links on default state on top of a light surface
- `$color-links-hovered-on-surface` — `#0f3d42` — Used for links on hover state on top of a light surface
- `$color-links-pressed-on-surface` — `#081f21` — Used for links on pressed state on top of a light surface
- `$color-links-disabled-on-surface` — `#6b6e70` — Used for links on a disabled state on top of a light surface

### Surface

- `$color-surface-primary` — `#ffffff` — Used for the fill color for elevated elements (menus
- `$color-surface-secondary` — `#f5f5f5` — Used for the fill color for elevated elements that needs a subtle contrast against the background…
- `$color-surface-tinted-1` — `#f2f7f7`
- `$color-surface-tinted-2` — `#e3ebed`
- `$color-surface-interactive-default` — `#ffffff` — Used for the fill color for default interactive items (e.g. Menu items
- `$color-surface-interactive-hovered` — `#f0f7f7` — Used for the fill color for interactive items on Hover state.
- `$color-surface-interactive-selected` — `#e0edf0` — Used for the fill color for interactive items on Selected state.
- `$color-surface-interactive-selected-hover` — `#d9e8ed`
- `$color-surface-interactive-disabled` — `#e8e8eb` — Used for the fill color for disabled interactive elements (e.g. a disabled option on a menu).

### Background

- `$color-background-accent-strongest` — `#0f3d42` — Used for the background fill or toast and darker elements.
- `$color-background-accent-strong` — `#1a5961` — Used for the background fill for sections that require a solid darker teal background.
- `$color-background-accent-solid` — `#2b7a87` — Use it as the background fill for components or sections that require the brand teal color.
- `$color-background-accent-subtle` — `#f0f7f7` — Use it as an alternative background fill for sections that require a subtle teal background (e.g full-width promo banners, hero sections, etc).
- `$color-background-default-solid` — `#ffffff` — Used for the main background fill for pages and sections.
- `$color-background-default-subtle` — `#f5f5f5` — Use it as an alternative background to create contrast with other cards or sections (e.g a Dashboard).
- `$color-background-default-static-black` — `#2e3030`
- `$color-background-default-static-white` — `#ffffff`
- `$color-background-alt-solid` — `#fce8bf` — Use it as an alternative background fill for sections that require a solid warm background (e.g full-width promo banners, hero sections, etc).
- `$color-background-alt-subtle` — `#faf5eb` — Use it as an alternative background fill for sections that require a subtle warm background (e.g full-width promo banners, hero sections, etc).
- `$color-background-success-solid` — `#298538` — Color background for solid success elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-success-base` — `#b2f2ba` — Middle color background for subtle success elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-success-subtle` — `#edfcf2` — Color background for subtle success elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-info-solid` — `#1c78cc` — Color background for solid info elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-info-base` — `#a6d9ff` — Middle color background for subtle info elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-info-subtle` — `#f0f7ff` — Color background for subtle info elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-warning-solid` — `#c74f00` — Color background for solid warning elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-warning-base` — `#ffd9a8` — Middle color background for subtle warning elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-warning-subtle` — `#fff5e5` — Color background for subtle warning elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-error-solid` — `#a81400` — Color background for solid error elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-error-base` — `#ffcfc9` — Middle color background for subtle error elements (tags, containers).
- `$color-background-error-subtle` — `#fff7f5` — Color background for subtle error elements (tags, containers).

### Icon

- `$color-icon-default-primary` — `#2e3030` — Used for isolated icons with higher hierarchy
- `$color-icon-default-primary-inverted` — `#ffffff`
- `$color-icon-default-secondary` — `#45474a` — Used for icon buttons on default state.
- `$color-icon-default-tertiary` — `#6b6e70` — Used for less important icons
- `$color-icon-default-disabled` — `#a3a6a8` — Used for icons on a disabled state.
- `$color-icon-default-disabled-on-surface` — `#6b6e70`
- `$color-icon-accent-default` — `#2b7a87` — Used for highlight icons and icon buttons on hover state.
- `$color-icon-accent-on-surface` — `#1a5961`
- `$color-icon-accent-high-contrast` — `#0f3d42`
- `$color-icon-accent-default-inverted` — `#e0edf0`
- `$color-icon-accent-on-surface-inverted` — `#d9e8ed`
- `$color-icon-accent-high-contrast-inverted` — `#bfe3e8`
- `$color-icon-error-default` — `#cc1700` — Used for accompany helper texts in a component's error messages.
- `$color-icon-error-high-contrast` — `#7a0f00` — Use it to accompany helper texts in a component's error messages that require higher contrast (e.g. a tinted red surface).
- `$color-icon-error-high-contrast-inverted` — `#ff9487` — Use it to accompany helper texts in a component's error messages on inverted surface.
- `$color-icon-info-default` — `#1c78cc` — Used for informational icons.
- `$color-icon-info-high-contrast` — `#124a7d` — Use for informational icons on lighter blue surfaces.
- `$color-icon-info-high-contrast-inverted` — `#73bffc` — Use for informational icons on inverted surfaces.
- `$color-icon-warning-default` — `#c74f00` — Used for warning icons.
- `$color-icon-warning-high-contrast` — `#803300` — Use for warning icons on lighter orange surfaces.
- `$color-icon-warning-high-contrast-inverted` — `#ffa84d` — Use for warning icons on lighter orange surfaces.
- `$color-icon-success-default` — `#298538` — Used for success icons.
- `$color-icon-success-high-contrast` — `#1c5929` — Use for success icons on lighter green surfaces.
- `$color-icon-success-high-contrast-inverted` — `#69db7d`
- `$color-icon-emphasis-default` — `#73296e` — Used for emphasis icons.
- `$color-icon-emphasis-high-contrast` — `#541c4f` — Use for emphasis icons on lighter purple surfaces.
- `$color-icon-subtle-primary` — `#b2bdbd` — Used for subtle icons.
- `$color-icon-subtle-secondary` — `#8f9c9c` — Used for subtle icons on hover states.
- `$color-icon-subtle-disabled` — `#c9cccf` — Used for subtle icons when disabled.

### Overlays

- `$color-overlays-dark` — `#00000080` — This is used for the dim fill overlay on modals and other panels that require the user's full attention.
- `$color-overlays-light` — `#ffffffb8` — Use for the background blur effect.

## Numeric Tokens (`build/scss/_numeric-tokens.scss`)

### Spacing

- `$spacing-none` — `0px` — (0px). Use when elements should have no gap or padding between them (e.g both consecutive sections have white bg, so the next section will have a 0px top padding to avoid an excessive spacing).
- `$spacing-2xs` — `4px` — (4px). Use for very tight spacing like icon-to-text gaps or subtle padding.
- `$spacing-xs` — `8px` — (8px). Use for compact component internal spacing or small gaps between related elements.
- `$spacing-sm` — `12px` — (12px). Use for moderate padding within components or gaps between closely related items.
- `$spacing-md` — `16px` — (16px). Default spacing for most component padding and gaps. Our baseline spacing unit.
- `$spacing-lg` — `24px` — (24px). Use for generous component padding or gaps between distinct sections.
- `$spacing-xl` — `32px` — (32px). Use for significant separation between components or section padding.
- `$spacing-2xl` — `40px` — (40px). Use for separation between content blocks within a section.
- `$spacing-3xl` — `48px` — (48px). Use for vertical separation between sections.
- `$spacing-4xl` — `64px` — (64px). Use for maximum spacing between distinct page areas or special layout needs.

### Radius

- `$radius-square` — `0px` — Sharp corners with no rounding (0px). Use for rectangular elements requiring hard edges.
- `$radius-sm` — `6px` — Small corner radius (6px). Use for subtle rounding on cards and other UI elements.
- `$radius-md` — `8px` — Medium corner radius (8px). Standard rounding for most components like inputs
- `$radius-lg` — `24px` — Large corner radius (24px). Use for Search Typeahead menus and pill-shaped items (e.g. Quantity Stepper).
- `$radius-xl` — `32px` — Extra-large corner radius (32px). Use for Sheet top corners and decorative large-surface use cases.
- `$radius-full` — `1000px` — Fully rounded borders for pill-shaped and circular elements. Browsers clamp border-radius to min(width, height) / 2, so this value produces a circle on square elements and a pill on rectangles.

### Size

- `$size-height-xs` — `24px` — (24px). Use as the base icon height and smaller elements.
- `$size-height-sm` — `32px` — (32px). Use it as the fixed height for Small Buttons and sm variants.
- `$size-height-md` — `40px` — (40px). Fixed height for default Chips (md)..
- `$size-height-lg` — `48px` — (48px). Fixed height for Large Buttons and other large component variants (lg).
- `$size-height-xl` — `72px` — (72px). Height for xl icon buttons and chips.
- `$size-height-icon-xs` — `16px` — (16px). Height for the smallest icon size (Small Button)
- `$size-height-icon-sm` — `20px` — (20px). Height for the icon in a Large Button.
- `$size-height-icon-md` — `24px` — (24px). Default size for most standalone icons and instances in icon buttons.
- `$size-height-icon-lg` — `32px` — (32px). Default size for most standalone icons and instances in icon buttons.
- `$size-height-icon-2xl` — `64px` — (64px). Default size for most standalone icons and instances in icon buttons.
- `$size-height-icon-4xl` — `80px` — (80px). Max height of a highlighted icon (e.g. a more detailed icon of a feature of benefit on a landing page).
- `$size-width-button-mw-lg` — `128px` — (128px). Ensures large buttons have substantial clickable area and prominent visual hierarchy.
- `$size-width-button-mw-sm` — `96px` — (96px). Ensures small buttons have adequate clickable area and visual presence.

## Elevation Tokens (`build/scss/_elevation.scss`)

- `$elevation-component-primary-button` — `0px 1px 2px 0px #00000066, 0px 4px 6px 0px #00000026` — $elevation-component-primary-button — Use it for Primary buttons.
- `$elevation-component-header` — `0px 4px 10px 0px #0b191c0d` — $elevation-component-header — Header nav bar.
- `$elevation-component-bottom-fixed` — `0px -6px 16px 0px #0b191c14` — $elevation-component-bottom-fixed — Bottom sheets, fixed elements to the bottom.
- `$elevation-component-bottom-sticky-bar` — `0px -4px 10px 0px #0b191c0d` — $elevation-component-bottom-sticky-bar — Product details sticky bar.
- `$elevation-sm` — `0px 0px 0px 1px #2121210f, 0px 4px 4px 0px #21212105, 0px 3px 3px 0px #2121210a, 0px 1px 1px 0px #2121210f, 0px 1px 1px 0px #21212114, 0px 8px 20px -6px #21212114` — $elevation-sm — Subtle elevation, tooltips, cards.
- `$elevation-md` — `0px 0px 0px 1px #2121210f, 0px 8px 8px 0px #21212105, 0px 5px 5px 0px #2121210a, 0px 3px 3px 0px #2121210f, 0px 1px 1px 0px #21212114, 0px 0px 0px 0px #2121211a` — $elevation-md — Dropdown menus.
- `$elevation-lg` — `0px 0px 0px 1px #2121210f, 0px 16px 16px 0px #21212105, 0px 10px 10px 0px #2121210a, 0px 6px 6px 0px #2121210f, 0px 3px 3px 0px #21212114, 0px 1px 1px 0px #2121211a` — $elevation-lg — Surfaced elements, panels.
- `$elevation-xl` — `0px 0px 0px 1px #2121210f, 0px 32px 32px 0px #21212105, 0px 20px 20px 0px #2121210a, 0px 12px 12px 0px #2121210f, 0px 5px 5px 0px #21212114, 0px 1px 1px 0px #2121211a` — $elevation-xl — Modals.

## Font Tokens (`build/scss/_fonts.scss`)

- `$fonts-primary-name` — `'Questa'`
- `$fonts-primary-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Questa_Regular.otf'`
- `$fonts-primary-src-0-format` — `'opentype'`
- `$fonts-primary-fallbacks-0` — `'Times New Roman'`
- `$fonts-primary-fallbacks-1` — `Times`
- `$fonts-primary-fallbacks-2` — `serif`
- `$fonts-primary-variants-0-weight` — `300`
- `$fonts-primary-variants-0-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Questa_Light.otf'`

- `$fonts-secondary-variants-1-src-0-format` — `'woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-1-src-1-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-Medium.woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-1-src-1-format` — `'woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-2-weight` — `600`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-2-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-SemiBold.woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-2-src-0-format` — `'woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-2-src-1-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-SemiBold.woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-2-src-1-format` — `'woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-3-weight` — `700`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-3-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-Bold.woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-3-src-0-format` — `'woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-3-src-1-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-Bold.woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-3-src-1-format` — `'woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-4-weight` — `800`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-4-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-ExtraBold.woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-4-src-0-format` — `'woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-4-src-1-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-ExtraBold.woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-4-src-1-format` — `'woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-5-weight` — `900`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-5-src-0-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-Black.woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-5-src-0-format` — `'woff2'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-5-src-1-url` — `'~design-system/assets/fonts/Lexend-Black.woff'`
- `$fonts-secondary-variants-5-src-1-format` — `'woff'`
- `$font-family-primary` — `Questa` — Use it for the primary typography styles (Displays, headings).
- `$font-family-secondary` — `Lexend` — Use it for the secondary typography styles (labels, body copy, captions, etc).
- `$font-weight-regular` — `400`
- `$font-weight-medium` — `500`
- `$font-weight-bold` — `700`
- `$text-transform-uppercase` — `uppercase` — Use it only for the Eyebrow typography style.
- `$text-transform-none` — `none`
- `$text-decoration-underline` — `underline` — Use it for links (text-underline styles).
- `$text-decoration-line-through` — `line-through` — Use it for the discounted price (text-strikethrough styles).
- `$text-decoration-none` — `none`

## Responsive / Desktop (`build/scss/_responsive-desktop.scss`)

### Typography

- `$desktop-typography-display-xl` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-display-xl;` — $desktop-typography-display-xl — Use for the largest display text in hero sections and major headings.
- `$desktop-typography-display-lg` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-display-lg;` — $desktop-typography-display-lg — Use for large display text in prominent hero sections.
- `$desktop-typography-display-md` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-display-md;` — $desktop-typography-display-md — Use for medium display text in featured sections and banners.
- `$desktop-typography-display-sm` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-display-sm;` — $desktop-typography-display-sm — Use for smaller display text in secondary hero sections.
- `$desktop-typography-display-xs` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-display-xs;` — $desktop-typography-display-xs — Use for the smallest display text in tertiary featured content.
- `$desktop-typography-headline-xl` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-headline-xl;` — $desktop-typography-headline-xl — Use for extra large section headlines and page titles.
- `$desktop-typography-headline-lg` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-headline-lg;` — $desktop-typography-headline-lg — Use for large section headlines and major content groupings.
- `$desktop-typography-headline-md` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-headline-md;` — $desktop-typography-headline-md — Use for medium section headlines and subsection titles.
- `$desktop-typography-headline-sm` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-headline-sm;` — $desktop-typography-headline-sm — Use for small section headlines and card titles.
- `$desktop-typography-price-lg` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-lg;` — $desktop-typography-price-lg — Use to display current product’s PDP.
- `$desktop-typography-price-lg-strikethrough` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-lg-strikethrough;` — $desktop-typography-price-lg-strikethrough — Use for display previous product’s price on PDP.
- `$desktop-typography-price-md` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-md;` — $desktop-typography-price-md — Use to display current product’s price on standard product cards.
- `$desktop-typography-price-md-strikethrough` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-md-strikethrough;` — $desktop-typography-price-md-strikethrough — Use to display previous product’s price on standard product cards.
- `$desktop-typography-price-sm` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-sm;` — $desktop-typography-price-sm — Use for small pricing display in compact layouts.
- `$desktop-typography-price-sm-strikethrough` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-sm-strikethrough;` — $desktop-typography-price-sm-strikethrough — Use for small crossed-out original prices in compact views.
- `$desktop-typography-price-xs` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-xs;` — $desktop-typography-price-xs — Use for the smallest pricing display in compact layouts.
- `$desktop-typography-price-xs-strikethrough` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-price-xs-strikethrough;` — $desktop-typography-price-xs-strikethrough — Use for the smallest crossed-out original prices in compact views.
- `$desktop-typography-text-xl-regular` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-xl-regular;` — $desktop-typography-text-xl-regular  — Use for extra large body text in introductory paragraphs.
- `$desktop-typography-text-xl-bold` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-xl-bold;` — $desktop-typography-text-xl-bold — Use for emphasized extra large body text.
- `$desktop-typography-text-xl-underline` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-xl-underline;` — $desktop-typography-text-xl-underline — Use for underlined extra large text links.
- `$desktop-typography-text-lg-regular` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-lg-regular;` — $desktop-typography-text-lg-regular — Use for large body text in feature descriptions.
- `$desktop-typography-text-lg-bold` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-lg-bold;` — $desktop-typography-text-lg-bold — Use for emphasized large body text and subheadings.
- `$desktop-typography-text-lg-underline` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-lg-underline;` — $desktop-typography-text-lg-underline — Use for underlined large text links.
- `$desktop-typography-text-md-regular` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-md-regular;` — $desktop-typography-text-md-regular — Use for standard body text and paragraph content.
- `$desktop-typography-text-md-bold` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-md-bold;` — $desktop-typography-text-md-bold — Use for emphasized medium body text and inline headings.
- `$desktop-typography-text-md-underline` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-md-underline;` — $desktop-typography-text-md-underline — Use for underlined medium text links.
- `$desktop-typography-text-sm-regular` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-sm-regular;` — $desktop-typography-text-sm-regular — Use for small body text captions and secondary information.
- `$desktop-typography-text-sm-bold` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-sm-bold;` — $desktop-typography-text-sm-bold — Use for emphasized small text and labels.
- `$desktop-typography-text-sm-underline` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-text-sm-underline;` — $desktop-typography-text-sm-underline — Use for underlined small text links and footnotes.
- `$desktop-typography-utility-cta-md` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-utility-cta-md;` — $desktop-typography-utility-cta-md — Use for for lg buttons.
- `$desktop-typography-utility-cta-sm` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-utility-cta-sm;` — $desktop-typography-utility-cta-sm — Use for sm buttons.
- `$desktop-typography-utility-eyebrow` — typography composite — use `@include desktop-typography-utility-eyebrow;` — $desktop-typography-utility-eyebrow — Use for small uppercase labels above headlines and sections.

### Section Padding

- `$desktop-section-padding-vertical-xl` — `128px` — Use for extra large vertical spacing in hero and feature sections.
- `$desktop-section-padding-vertical-lg` — `80px` — Use for default vertical spacing (top and bottom padding) in major sections.
- `$desktop-section-padding-vertical-md` — `64px` — Use for medium vertical spacing in standard sections.
- `$desktop-section-padding-vertical-sm` — `56px` — Use for small vertical spacing in compact sections.
- `$desktop-section-padding-vertical-xs` — `40px` — Use for extra small vertical spacing in tight layouts. Apply to top/bottom padding for adjacent sections with white bg.

### Typescale

- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-xs` — `12px` — Text sm.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-sm` — `14px` — Text sm.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-md` — `16px` — Text md.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-lg` — `18px` — Text lg.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-xl` — `22px` — Text xl.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-2xl` — `24px` — Headline sm.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-3xl` — `28px` — Headline md.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-4xl` — `32px` — Headline lg.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-5xl` — `36px` — Headline xl.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-6xl` — `40px` — Headline 2xl.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-7xl` — `48px` — Display xs.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-8xl` — `64px` — Display sm.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-9xl` — `80px` — Display md.
- `$desktop-typescale-font-size-10xl` — `104px` — Display lg.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-3xl` — `34px` — Headline md.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-4xl` — `38px` — Headline lg.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-5xl` — `44px` — Headline xl.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-6xl` — `48px` — Headline 2xl.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-7xl` — `48px` — Display xs.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-8xl` — `64px` — Display sm.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-9xl` — `80px` — Display md.
- `$desktop-typescale-line-height-10xl` — `104px` — Display lg.

### Section Padding

- `$mobile-section-padding-vertical-lg` — `64px` — Use for default vertical spacing (top and bottom padding) in major sections.
- `$mobile-section-padding-vertical-md` — `48px` — Use for medium vertical spacing in standard sections.
- `$mobile-section-padding-vertical-sm` — `40px` — Use for small vertical spacing in compact sections.
- `$mobile-section-padding-vertical-xs` — `32px` — Use for extra small vertical spacing in tight layouts. Apply to top/bottom padding for adjacent sections with white bg.

### Typescale

- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-xs` — `12px` — Text sm.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-sm` — `14px` — Text sm.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-md` — `16px` — Text md.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-lg` — `18px` — Text lg.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-xl` — `20px` — Text xl.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-2xl` — `22px` — Headline sm.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-3xl` — `24px` — Headline md.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-4xl` — `28px` — Headline lg.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-5xl` — `32px` — Headline xl.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-6xl` — `36px` — Headline 2xl.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-7xl` — `40px` — Display xs.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-8xl` — `48px` — Display sm.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-9xl` — `56px` — Display md.
- `$mobile-typescale-font-size-10xl` — `64px` — Display lg.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-3xl` — `28px` — Headline md.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-4xl` — `34px` — Headline lg.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-5xl` — `38px` — Headline xl.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-6xl` — `44px` — Headline 2xl.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-7xl` — `40px` — Display xs.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-8xl` — `48px` — Display sm.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-9xl` — `56px` — Display md.
- `$mobile-typescale-line-height-10xl` — `64px` — Display lg.

- `$container-regular-max-width` — `1440`
- `$container-wide-padding` — `32px`
- `$container-wide-gap` — `32px`
- `$container-wide-max-width` — `1600`
- `$container-narrow-padding` — `64px`

## Breakpoint Tokens (`build/scss/_breakpoints.scss`)

- `$breakpoints-xs` — `360` — 360-575px - Phones
- `$breakpoints-sm` — `576` — 576-767px - Phablets and small tablets
- `$breakpoints-md` — `768` — 768-991px - Tablets, windowed display
- `$breakpoints-lg` — `992` — 992-1199px - Tablets, windowed display
- `$breakpoints-xl` — `1200` — 1200-1727px - Computers, laptops, premium tablets, folding phones (HD/FHD)
- `$breakpoints-xxl` — `1728` — 1728+ - Computers, TVs, laptops, premium tablets (FHD, 2K, 4K, 5K, 8K)

## Ratio Tokens (`build/scss/_ratios.scss`)

### Vertical

- `$vertical-2-3` — `'2 / 3'` — Vertical 2:3 aspect ratio.
- `$vertical-9-16` — `'9 / 16'` — Vertical 9:16 aspect ratio.
- `$vertical-3-4` — `'3 / 4'` — Vertical 3:4 aspect ratio.
- `$vertical-1-1` — `'1 / 1'` — Square aspect ratio. Default illustrations, product or recipe thumbnail, also used for avatar pictures.

### Horizontal

- `$horizontal-3-2` — `'3 / 2'` — Horizontal 3:2 aspect ratio. Use it for illustrations or images on mobile, it's not restricted for desktop either.
- `$horizontal-16-9` — `'16 / 9'` — Horizontal 16:9 aspect ratio. Use it for featured images or product shots (mostly sets) where the cropping area affects visibility of the entire image.
- `$horizontal-4-3` — `'4 / 3'` — Horizontal 4:3. Default use for single images on Hero sections.
- `$horizontal-1-1` — `'1 / 1'` — Square aspect ratio. Default illustrations, product or recipe thumbnail, also used for avatar pictures.

## Focus Tokens (`build/scss/_focus.scss`)

- `$focus-default` — `0px 0px 0px 2px #ffffff, 0px 0px 0px 6px #2e3030` — Use for default focus ring.
- `$focus-default-inverted` — `0px 0px 0px 2px #2e3030, 0px 0px 0px 6px #bfe3e8` — Use for default focus ring on inverted surfaces.
- `$focus-accent` — `0px 0px 0px 2px #ffffff, 0px 0px 0px 6px #2b7a87` — Use for the focus ring of highlighted components.
- `$focus-error` — `0px 0px 0px 2px #ffffff, 0px 0px 0px 6px #7a0f00` — Use for the focus ring of error state components.
- `$focus-error-inverted` — `0px 0px 0px 2px #2e3030, 0px 0px 0px 6px #fa6652` — Use for the focus ring of error state components on inverted surfaces.

## Motion Tokens (`build/scss/_motion.scss`)

### Delays

- `$delays-expressive-lg` — `180ms`
- `$delays-expressive-md` — `140ms`
- `$delays-expressive-sm` — `100ms`
- `$delays-expressive-xl` — `220ms`
- `$delays-expressive-xs` — `60ms`
- `$delays-expressive-xxl` — `260ms`
- `$delays-productive-lg` — `90ms`
- `$delays-productive-md` — `70ms`
- `$delays-productive-sm` — `50ms`
- `$delays-productive-xl` — `110ms`
- `$delays-productive-xs` — `30ms`
- `$delays-productive-xxl` — `130ms`

### Durations

- `$durations-expressive-lg` — `900ms`
- `$durations-expressive-md` — `700ms`
- `$durations-expressive-sm` — `500ms`
- `$durations-expressive-xl` — `1100ms`
- `$durations-expressive-xs` — `300ms`
- `$durations-expressive-xxl` — `1300ms`
- `$durations-productive-lg` — `450ms`
- `$durations-productive-md` — `350ms`
- `$durations-productive-sm` — `250ms`
- `$durations-productive-xl` — `550ms`
- `$durations-productive-xs` — `150ms`
- `$durations-productive-xxl` — `650ms`

### Easings

- `$easings-direct-direct-curve` — `cubic-bezier(0.1, 0.25, 0.25, 1)`
- `$easings-indirect-enter-curve` — `cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.6, 0.25, 1)`
- `$easings-indirect-exit-curve` — `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.75, 0)`
- `$easings-indirect-linear-curve` — `cubic-bezier(0, 0, 1, 1)`
- `$easings-indirect-standard-curve` — `cubic-bezier(0.5, 0, 0, 1)`

---

## Token Mapping Tips

1. **Always use semantic tokens first** — Prefer `$color-buttons-primary-default` over primitive values. Primitives are generated but intentionally excluded from `build/scss/index.scss` and should never be consumed directly in product code.
2. **Check for state variants** — Most interactive elements have `default`, `hovered`, `pressed`, and `disabled` states.
3. **Typography tokens are composite** — They bundle `font-family`, `font-size`, `font-weight`, `line-height`, and `letter-spacing`. Use the matching `@mixin` rather than referencing the raw token.
4. **Spacing follows t-shirt sizing** — Use the scale consistently (`xs`, `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`).
5. **Inverted tokens on dark backgrounds** — Tokens ending in `-inverted` are paired with inverted surfaces.
6. **Border radius consistency** — Stick to `sm` (6px) or `md` (8px) for most UI elements.
7. **Icon sizes align with heights** — Match icon size tokens to component height tokens.
8. **Motion** — Use `productive` durations for UI interactions, `expressive` for storytelling moments.

---

## SCSS Variable Access

In SCSS files, import the generated index and reference tokens with the `$` prefix:

```scss
@import '~build/scss/index';

.my-component {
  color: $color-text-default-primary;
  padding: $spacing-md;
  background: $color-surface-primary;
  transition: all $durations-productive-sm $easings-direct-direct-curve;
}

// Typography composites use their matching mixin:
.hero-heading {
  @include desktop-typography-headline-xl;
}
```

## TypeScript / JavaScript Documentation

In component props docs and comments, reference tokens with the Web Code Syntax format:

```tsx
/**
 * Typography: $desktop-typography-text-md-regular (Lexend Regular, 16px, 150% line-height)
 * Icon Size: $size-height-icon-sm (20px)
 * Spacing: $spacing-md (16px)
 * Elevation: $elevation-md (multi-layer card shadow)
 */
```
